title: Meshtastic LongFast point-to-point between two Hwange ranger camps
summary: Two ranger camps in Hwange — Shumba and Mandavu — sit ~10 km apart with low scrub and a small ridge between them. Op A computes the link budget, terrain path profile, and Fresnel-zone clearance for a Meshtastic LongFast 915 MHz link between portable nodes mounted at typical operator height (2.5 m AGL on a portable mast). The result tells the field ops lead whether the two camps are LongFast-direct, need an intermediate repeater on the intervening ridge, or should fall back to a known-degraded SF12 store-and-forward path until repeater placement is funded.
demonstrates: Op A (§4.0) point-to-point with terrain + Fresnel artifacts
lora link-type plugin: SF planning end-to-end on a single link, not raster (§4.6)
meshtastic-node-915-tx seed equipment profile (LongFast SF11/250 kHz)

sites.name: ranger-camp-shumba
sites.notes: Existing camp at Shumba picnic area.

notes: Symmetric link — both ends carry the same node configuration (LongFast SF11, 22 dBm, 3 dBi vertical). Reciprocity holds; the link_budget reports a single number good for either direction. If the result returns received_power below the radio's -130 dBm sensitivity, the field ops decision is to add a relay node on the intervening ridge — the rendered_cross_section artifact tells the operator exactly where on the path the dominant obstruction sits, which is the input for siting that relay. This is the only Op A scenario in the seed library that exercises the LoRa plugin (boundary-rtk-survey covers Op A on the rtk plugin).
